_README: Copy this file to ~/Library/Application Support/Aguacate/credentials.json and fill in YOUR OWN public client IDs. These are PUBLIC client identifiers for the PKCE flow — Aguacate never uses or stores OAuth client secrets on the device (C1). Never commit a real credentials.json.

google._note: Google Cloud Console → Credentials → OAuth client ID → type 'Desktop app'. Desktop clients use PKCE; the generated 'client secret' is not used or needed by Aguacate.

microsoft._note: Azure Portal → App registrations → new public client. Enable 'Allow public client flows'. Aguacate uses the device-code flow; no secret involved.
